title: rubus × scandens
intro: Rubus × scandens, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between the red thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the black thimbleberry (Rubus argutus). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × scandens include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Rubus × scandens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is often used in combination with other herbs to create a range of herbal preparations.
